title: Obstructive sleep apnea anesthesia review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms, sleep-study results, and severity documentation.
assessment.rationale: The exact ASA section supports record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, previous airway difficulty, OSA symptoms, sleep-study results, and severity documentation without inferring OSA or management.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for the Perioperative Management of Patients with Obstructive Sleep Apnea
source.population: Adult and pediatric patients older than 1 year with suspected or established obstructive sleep apnea in perioperative settings.
source.setting: Preoperative, intraoperative, and postoperative anesthesia care.
source.exclusions: Exact for record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms and studies, severity, procedure, and opioid-related risk context. Treatment and disposition recommendations are not mapped.

exact_location.heading: Preoperative evaluation for suspected or established OSA

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms, sleep-study results, and severity documentation.

assessment.text: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.
assessment.rationale: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Obstructive sleep apnea anesthesia review; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

sources_used.title: Practice Guidelines for the Perioperative Management of Patients with Obstructive Sleep Apnea
